output.summary: Hero.css defines `.top-gradient` as a global (unscoped) selector. PostHero and EventHero both add the `hero` class to their wrapper and use `<div class="top-gradient">` and `<div class="hero-inner">` internally, relying on Hero.css for gradient styling. To make each block fully independent: (1) scope Hero.css styles under `.hero` so they only apply to Hero block instances, (2) create PostHero.css with its own scoped `.post-hero .top-gradient` styles, (3) create EventHero.css with its own scoped `.event-hero .top-gradient` styles, (4) remove the `hero` class from PostHero and EventHero wrappers so they no longer depend on Hero's class namespace.

fileChanges.description: Scope `.top-gradient` under `.hero` to prevent style leaking to other blocks
fileChanges.details: Change the selector from `.top-gradient` to `.hero .top-gradient`. This ensures the gradient styles only apply when `.top-gradient` is a descendant of an element with the `.hero` class -- which is the Hero block's own wrapper. After this change, PostHero and EventHero will no longer inherit these styles (which is the desired outcome -- they will get their own copies).

fileChanges.description: Create dedicated CSS for PostHero with its own scoped `.top-gradient` styles
fileChanges.details: Create a new file `blocks/PostHero/PostHero.css` containing `.post-hero .top-gradient { ... }` with the same gradient rules currently in Hero.css. This gives PostHero full ownership of its gradient styling. The gradient values should be identical to Hero.css (same color-mix, radial-gradient values) since the visual output should remain the same.

fileChanges.description: Create dedicated CSS for EventHero with its own scoped `.top-gradient` styles
fileChanges.details: Create a new file `blocks/EventHero/EventHero.css` containing `.event-hero .top-gradient { ... }` with the same gradient rules currently in Hero.css. This gives EventHero full ownership of its gradient styling.

fileChanges.description: Remove the `hero` class from PostHero's wrapper element to decouple from Hero block
fileChanges.details: On line 28, change `'post-hero hero relative overflow-visible'` to `'post-hero relative overflow-visible'`. The `hero` class is Hero block's namespace and PostHero should not use it. PostHero already has its own `post-hero` class. Also on line 38, change `'hero-inner relative'` to `'post-hero-inner relative'` to avoid sharing class names with Hero. Note: `hero-inner` is not styled in Hero.css (it's only used as an HTML structure class with inline Tailwind), so this rename is purely for namespace clarity and has no visual impact.

fileChanges.description: Remove the `hero` class from EventHero's wrapper element to decouple from Hero block
fileChanges.details: On line 77, change `'event-hero hero relative overflow-visible'` to `'event-hero relative overflow-visible'`. Also on line 83, change `'hero-inner relative'` to `'event-hero-inner relative'` for namespace clarity. Same rationale as PostHero.

designDecisions.evidence: User requested Hero block styles be scoped so they don't leak to other blocks. `.top-gradient` is currently a global selector that PostHero and EventHero inherit implicitly.

designDecisions.decision: Create separate CSS files for PostHero and EventHero with identical gradient styles scoped to their own class
designDecisions.evidence: User requested each block own its own styles. PostHero and EventHero currently have no CSS files and depend on Hero.css for `.top-gradient` styling.

designDecisions.decision: Remove `hero` class from PostHero and EventHero wrapper elements
designDecisions.evidence: User requested full independence. PostHero uses `post-hero hero` and EventHero uses `event-hero hero` as wrapper classes. The `hero` class belongs to Hero block's namespace.

designDecisions.decision: Rename `hero-inner` to `post-hero-inner` and `event-hero-inner` in respective blocks
designDecisions.evidence: Part of decoupling -- each block should use its own class namespace. `hero-inner` is not styled in CSS (only used structurally), so this is a namespace-only change.

output.qaNote: Verify: (1) Hero block on test page still renders gradient correctly, (2) PostHero on a single post page still renders gradient correctly, (3) EventHero on a single event page still renders gradient correctly. All three should look visually identical before and after the change. Also verify no other blocks reference `.top-gradient` or `.hero` class.
